name: eslint-plugin-security
description: Security-focused ESLint rules for detecting potential vulnerabilities
rationale: Already installed and configured. Provides detect-object-injection, detect-non-literal-fs-filename, and other security rules. Complements custom framework rules. Keep as-is.

name: eslint-plugin-n
description: ESLint rules for Node.js-specific best practices (successor to eslint-plugin-node)
rationale: Provides no-deprecated-api, no-missing-import, no-unsupported-features rules. Could catch Node.js API deprecations. However, current framework already uses typescript-eslint for import checking and custom framework rules for file I/O safety. Low marginal value until the framework targets multiple Node.js versions.

metadata.triggerReconsider: When framework needs to support multiple Node.js LTS versions simultaneously

name: eslint-plugin-jsonc
description: ESLint plugin for linting JSON, JSONC, and JSON5 files
rationale: Could lint JSON files and JSONL schema compliance. However, framework already uses Prettier for JSON formatting and Zod schemas for validation at runtime. Adding another JSON linter layer creates dual enforcement. Reconsider if JSON validation errors become frequent.

metadata.triggerReconsider: If JSON/JSONL formatting issues start slipping through Prettier + Zod

name: eslint-plugin-import-x
description: ESLint plugin for validating import/export syntax and module resolution (maintained fork of eslint-plugin-import)
rationale: Provides import order enforcement, no-cycle detection, no-unresolved checks. However, framework already uses madge for circular dependency detection and knip for unused exports. The import ordering value is cosmetic. Defer unless import-related bugs emerge.

metadata.triggerReconsider: If import resolution bugs or circular dependencies escape madge detection

name: filesystem-mcp
description: MCP server providing filesystem access for AI assistants
rationale: Claude Code already has native filesystem access via its built-in tools (Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep). An MCP server for filesystem operations would be redundant and add latency. No value for framework development.

name: git-mcp
description: MCP server providing Git operations for AI assistants
rationale: Claude Code has native git access via Bash tool. Git MCP would add indirection without benefit. The framework's hook system and pre-commit/pre-push scripts already handle git workflow automation.

name: context7
description: MCP server that provides up-to-date documentation context for libraries and frameworks
rationale: Provides live documentation lookup for libraries. Could be useful when working with Zod, ESLint plugin APIs, or other dependencies. However, Claude's training data already covers these well, and the framework has CLAUDE.md with project-specific context. Reconsider if documentation staleness becomes an issue with newer library versions.

metadata.triggerReconsider: When framework dependencies move to versions beyond Claude's training cutoff

name: sequential-thinking
description: MCP server for structured step-by-step reasoning and problem decomposition
rationale: Claude Opus 4.6 has strong built-in reasoning capabilities. The GSD workflow already provides structured planning and execution phases. An external sequential-thinking server would add complexity without meaningful improvement to reasoning quality.

name: actions/cache
description: GitHub Action for caching dependencies and build outputs to speed up workflows
rationale: Framework has 9 CI workflows. Caching node_modules between runs would reduce CI time significantly. Standard practice for Node.js projects. Low risk, high reward.

metadata.installPlan: Add cache step to CI workflows caching node_modules with package-lock.json hash key

name: github/codeql-action
description: GitHub Action for CodeQL semantic code analysis and vulnerability detection
rationale: Provides deep semantic security analysis beyond what ESLint catches. However, the framework already has eslint-plugin-security plus 23 custom framework rules covering security patterns. CodeQL adds significant CI time (5-15 min). Defer until the framework is consumed by multiple projects where broader security scanning justifies the overhead.

metadata.triggerReconsider: When framework is used in production projects handling sensitive data

name: dependabot
description: Automated dependency update PRs from GitHub
rationale: Framework has 10+ direct devDependencies that need version tracking. Dependabot creates automated PRs for security patches and version updates. Zero-config via dependabot.yml. Essential for long-lived projects.

metadata.installPlan: Create .github/dependabot.yml with npm ecosystem config, weekly schedule

name: reviewdog/action-eslint
description: GitHub Action that posts ESLint results as PR review comments
rationale: Posts ESLint findings as inline PR comments, improving code review experience. Upgraded from defer to adopt per user decision -- valuable for PR review workflow even with single contributor.

metadata.installPlan: Add reviewdog/action-eslint step to CI lint workflow with GITHUB_TOKEN

name: syncpack
description: Monorepo dependency version synchronization tool
rationale: Framework is a single-package repository, not a monorepo. Syncpack solves cross-package version drift which is not applicable here. No value for current architecture.

name: depcheck
description: Tool for analyzing unused dependencies in Node.js projects
rationale: Framework already uses knip for unused dependency and export detection. Knip is more comprehensive (checks exports, types, and config files, not just imports). Depcheck would be redundant.

name: license-checker
description: Tool for checking and reporting licenses of npm dependencies
rationale: Important for compliance when framework is distributed or consumed by enterprise projects. Currently the framework is private and single-user. Defer until distribution or enterprise adoption is planned.

metadata.triggerReconsider: When framework is published as an npm package or used in enterprise context

name: npm-check-updates
description: Tool for finding newer versions of npm dependencies
rationale: Useful for manual dependency updates, but dependabot (adopted above) handles this automatically with PRs. Manual ncu runs are redundant when dependabot is active. Reconsider only if dependabot is removed.

metadata.triggerReconsider: If dependabot is removed or insufficient for update tracking

name: commitlint
description: Lint commit messages against conventional commit format
rationale: Framework uses conventional-style commit messages in practice (feat, fix, chore prefixes) but does not strictly enforce them. Adding commitlint would formalize this. However, the primary committer is Claude Code which already follows the convention via GSD workflow. Defer until human contributors need guardrails.

metadata.triggerReconsider: When multiple human contributors are making direct commits

name: lefthook
description: Fast, cross-platform git hooks manager (alternative to husky)
rationale: Framework already uses husky which is well-integrated with the existing pre-commit and pre-push scripts. Switching hook managers would require migrating all hook configurations with no functional benefit. Husky is stable and sufficient.

name: changesets
description: Tool for managing versioning and changelogs in mono/multi-package repos
rationale: Changesets is designed for managing releases of published npm packages, especially in monorepos. The framework is a private, single-package project. TDMS already tracks changes via JSONL. No versioning/changelog automation needed at this stage.

name: turbo
description: High-performance build system for JavaScript/TypeScript monorepos
rationale: Turbo (Turborepo) optimizes task orchestration across multiple packages in a monorepo. The framework is a single package. The existing npm scripts handle all build/lint/test tasks adequately. No build orchestration complexity to solve.

name: actions/dependency-review-action
description: GitHub Action that flags vulnerable and license-risky dependencies in pull requests
rationale: Scans PR dependency changes for known vulnerabilities and license risks. Catches risky deps before they merge. Added per user decision as early adoption for PR review quality.

metadata.installPlan: Add dependency-review step to PR CI workflow, configure license allow-list
